result:

      "evidence": "Candidate LDIP-API-201-223 ('matter.created through audit.exported') = 23 IDs, but the no-omission matrix enumerates 24 events (matter.created + 9 document.* + clause.extracted + issue.created + dd_request.matched + search.executed + 4 agent.* + 2 output.* + clean_room.created + external_user.invited + share.revoked + audit.exported). Candidate LDIP-DOC-301-308 ('execution through unknown') = 8 IDs, but matrix enumerates 9 version labels (execution, final_clean, final_redline, board_approved, counterparty_mark_up, client_mark_up, internal_review, initial_draft, unknown). The dropped member is invisible at the candidates-ledger level because the cell uses span shorthand, not inline enumeration; only the matrix reveals the full set. The candidates doc is the stated conversion source ('must eventually be converted to a machine-readable requirement row'), so 1:1 conversion by span drops one event and the 'unknown' version label — the latter being the trigger for the manual-review failure default (LDIP-ING-201-207).",

    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-02-F2",
      "severity": "P2",
      "title": "ID-range cardinality mismatches where cell enumerates set but span is wrong (RBAC roles, approval workflows)",

LDIP-TOOL-301-307 = 7 IDs but matrix approval-workflow list enumerates only 6 (external share posting, client/counterparty email draft use, Word redline save, privileged material sharing, personal-data material sharing, Ethical Wall exception access) — span over-allocates by one or a 7th workflow is under-enumerated in the matrix.",

    },
    {
      "id": "C-LDIP-02-F3",

These are captured only by the single section candidate LDIP-DQ-003 (section 19, 1140-1172). Counts (11/10) differ from the coded NFRs DQ-001-007 (LDIP-DQ-101-107) and AIQ-001-008 (LDIP-DQ-201-208), so section-19 metrics are NOT redundantly covered — 21 named 'quality metric' objects lack stable per-item IDs for conversion/traceability.",

      "severity": "P3",
      "title": "Enumerable principle/format sets held only at section granularity",
      "evidence": "10 design goals (section 2.2) tracked only by LDIP-GOAL-003; 6 anti-patterns (section 2.3) only by LDIP-RISK-001; 6 agent-output format elements (section 18: evidence table, excluded data, uncertainty, recommended action, reviewer, approval status) only by LDIP-OUT-001. The risk register (LDIP-RISK-101-110) by contrast received per-item IDs.",

      "id": "C-LDIP-02-F5",

      "title": "Section-candidate summaries drift from authoritative matrix enumeration; named-object ranges omit source_lines",
      "evidence": "LDIP-CLEAN-003 required_behavior lists 8 policies (no-download default, watermark, expiry, purpose binding, no cross-room search, approval, revocation, post-access audit) and omits 'screenshot_warning', though LDIP-CLEAN-301-309 and the matrix both include it (9 policies).       "recommended_action": "Reconcile section-candidate summaries to the matrix enumerations and carry source_lines onto named-object ranges to make each row independently convertible."

  "summary": "Coverage is structurally complete: all 23 sections, all subsections, all 20 families, and all mandatory named-object classes are represented; exclusions/defers carry reasons; no premature implemented/production_ready status. One P1 (F1) — the conversion-authoritative candidates ledger undercounts events (23 vs 24) and version labels (8 vs 9) behind 'through' shorthand, risking omission of one event and the 'unknown' label at machine conversion — blocks the implementation gate until ranges are re-cardinalized. Planning gate is not blocked; the next artifact (ldip-rp-anchor-map.md) may proceed. F2/F3 P2 granularity gaps and F4/F5 P3 traceability items should be cleared alongside F1 before the implementation gate opens."
